summarization.a: This figure shows the etch rate versus activation-step duration for five materials. Al₂O₃, HfO₂, and AlNₓ all show a rise in etch rate with increasing duration. TiN remains near zero and only shifts slightly from a small negative value toward zero. SiO₂ increases much more strongly than the others, becoming the dominant curve at longer activation times.

a.question: How does the etch rate trend differ among the materials as the activation step duration increases?
a.answer: Al₂O₃, HfO₂, and AlNₓ all show a  shallow rise in etch rate as the activation step becomes increases. TiN stays close to zero throughout, only moving slightly upward from a negative value at short times. SiO₂  increases far more strongly than the other materials, becoming the most rapidly etched surface at longer activation durations.

a.question: Which material showsthe highest etch rate at the longest activation-step duration?
Does TiN transition into positive etch rates at any of the activation durations shown?
Which materials show a clear upward trend in etch rate between 5 and 20 seconds?
